content:
It's helpful to provide information when creating any question. The info you supplied is identical to the question.

What OS are you using ? What version of Thunderbird are you using ?

Are you talking about an IMAP Inbox or a Pop Inbox OR are you using 'Unified Folders' view option ? In which case are you talking about dragging from the 'unified Inbox' itself and not the actual Inbox of an account ?

Upload an image which shows the Inbox and the subfolder - is it a subfolder of the account or of the Inbox ?

Are you able to left click hold down on email in Inbox and drag email to hover over the subfolder - at this point what do you see - do you see the red circle with red line through it OR do you get the small outline of rectangle icon indicating you should be able to drop into that folder.

Does the subfolder Name show using italic font  ? if yes, then it cannot store emails, but it can contain folders that have emails.

content:
Pop accounts. Sent, Drafts etc are not created as folders until you create and send an email from that pop account.

Imap accounts You need to subscribe to see folders. In Folder Pane

  • Right click on the account name and select 'Subscribe'
  • select all required folders and click on OK


re : Account showing only Inbox.

I notice the account with only Inbox has a red icon with no padlock - so it looks like it's not using secure server settings.

In 'Account Settings' for the problem account

I'm not sure which is the problem account based on your image as the imap account shows info, but the one highlighted - blue - is collapsed. It might help if a portion of the account name was revealed so we know which is which. Please post useful images as described below. I've explained why we need the info.

In left pane is the name of the account and it will have a > chevron on the left of account name. click on that chevron to reveal all the option. When you post an image, the contents displaying below the name help to identify whether you have a Pop or Imap account.

Select 'Server Settings' for problem account Post image showing the all data on the right side. Just obscure the first part of email address, but nothing else. This will tell us what Server Type is set up for the account. Don't worry about server names because they are general very generic and used by thousands of people. Then click on 'Advanced' button - post an image showing what is selected. That will tell whether the account is set up as a deferred account using eg: Local Folders or whether it's set up to use it's own set of folders.

Select 'Copies & Folders' Post image showing the all data on the right side. Just obscure the first part of email address, but nothing else. This will tell whether all is set up correctly. If there are more secure settings available then we can advise.

content:
re :I will wager your next weeks pay that I have to change IMAP Mail Server to POP Mail Server. Am I right?

You are right if you want to use a Pop account. It would mean creating the account as new. But if you want to use Imap then you just alter the server settings.

You have created an Imap server type for that mail account, so would have to use imap server settings. You do not have to delete the account if you want to use it as an imap account. You would just need to alter the server settings.

If you had shown me the current server setting then I could have told you the correct imap settings. I'm going to guess it might be shaw / rogers.

Imap :

  • Incoming Mail Server: imap.shaw.ca
  • Port: 993
  • Connection Security: SSL/TLS
  • Authentication Method: Normal Password
  • Username: Shaw email address without the @shaw.ca

SMTP

  • Outgoing Mail Server: mail.shaw.ca
  • Outgoing Port: 587
  • Connection Security: STARTTLS
  • Authentication Method: Normal Password
  • Username: Shaw email address without the @shaw.ca

Most servers ask for user name = full email address, but it's possible if you use shaw then they may insist Username is the bit before the @.

If you do not want Imap then you would have to delete the Imap account and create a new account. Menu app > New account > Email Enter requested information Please note Thunderbird tends to auto search for the IMAP settings, so you would need to manually alter the Protocol to use POP and then pop settings get selected.

But these are the Pop server settings for shaw. POP

  • Incoming Server: pop.shaw.ca
  • Port: 995
  • Security Type: SSL/TLS
  • Authentication Method: Normal Password
  • Username: Shaw email address without the @shaw.ca

content: Try to create an exclusion in Windows Defender for the Thunderbird profile folder, so that the Defender's real-time scanner will not scan it. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/profiles-where-thunderbird-stores-user-data#w_how-to-find-your-profile
creator: christ1

content:
To exclude the TB folder: 

- click Start and type: virus 
- click Virus & threat protection System settings > Virus & threat protection settings > Manage settings 
- scroll down to Exclusions > click 'Add an exclusion' > Folder 
- navigate to and select the Thunderbird Profiles folder (all profiles - usually at C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Thunderbird\Profiles) > click 'Select Folder' 
   - to locate the Profiles folder: 
     - TB menu > Help > Troubleshooting Information  
     - Application Basics section > scroll down to 'Profile folder > click the 'Open Folder' button 
     - navigate up two levels  
IMPORTANT: go to TB menu > Settings > Privacy & Security > Security > Antivirus >

enable 'Allow antivirus clients to quarantine individual incoming messages'.
creator: siffemoz
